mnemonics: CID for CIA: Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability are the core principles. Remember, a good secret agent (CIA) needs proper Clearance (Confidentiality), can't have his Dossier altered (Integrity), and must be Available for the mission.
keyPoints: Confidentiality: Ensuring information is accessible only to those authorized.
Integrity: Safeguarding the accuracy and completeness of information and processing methods.
Availability: Ensuring authorized users have access to information and associated assets when required.
commonMistakes: Focusing only on confidentiality (secrecy) and neglecting integrity (data tampering) and availability (denial-of-service).
Confusing integrity with confidentiality (e.g., encrypted data can be confidential but still be altered, violating integrity).
